description: IDC deterministic-core hooks (v4). SessionEnd: the recirculation-intake safety-net sweep (re-stages rogue Buildables, captures untickered discovery/deferral markers). Stop: the fixpoint gate — an autorun/build orchestrator DRAIN session (self-gated via the ledger's session-scoped orchestrator_drain marker) cannot stop while `idc_autorun_drain.py` reports `drain: recirc-pending` (exit 4) AND the ledger agrees work remains; the ledger alone never blocks a clean board (drain: complete wins), bounded N=3 then loud-fail + a one-time board annotation. SubagentStop: (1) the verdict gate — a review agent cannot stop without a validated verdict JSON at docs/workflow/code-reviews/ (bounded N=3, then loud-fail); (2) the recirculator closeout-or-checkpoint detective — when the recirculator subagent stops mid-drain WITHOUT a valid idc_recirc_closeout.py closeout, every still-open Stage=Recirculation ∧ Status=Todo inbox ticket gets a resume-checkpoint comment {branch, PR#, dispositions} reconstructed deterministically from the agent transcript + a recirc_checkpoint:<ticket> ledger taint (drop F); a valid closeout allows + clears the taints. Post-hoc detective — fail-OPEN, never blocks the stop; records under observe-only. PreToolUse (Bash/Write/Edit): the shared Path Gate transport — raw `gh issue create` / `gh pr merge` / `gh issue close` / state-closing or dependency `gh api` (`dependencies/blocked_by` POST/DELETE) / raw board mutation (`gh project item-edit|item-add|item-delete`, GraphQL board/issue writes) that bypasses the transition engine / finisher tail, INCLUDING one hidden behind interpreter indirection (`bash|sh|zsh FILE`, `source`/`.` FILE, a quoted `bash -c` payload — resolved by bounded, cycle-guarded, sensitive-file-refusing inspection), OR a direct Write/Edit repository mutation without a live authorization, is a HARD DENY; IDC_HOOKS_OBSERVE_ONLY=1 downgrades any deny. PostToolUse (Bash): two fail-OPEN, ALWAYS board-coherence self-repair observers (v4 Phase 3 Stage D) — (1) commit-sync: after a successful `git commit`, if the commit's linked board item (derived from the commit message / branch name — no board GraphQL) isn't In Progress, auto-repairs it via `idc_transition.py claim` or injects the exact remediation; (2) issue-create-add: after a successful `gh issue create`, if the same command didn't also add the new issue to the board with Stage+Status, injects the exact remediation. Both never block (additionalContext injection only, never decision:block) and never fire a live board scan. UserPromptExpansion (matched to idc: commands): the command entry gate — before a governed /idc:* command expands, it binds the running plugin runtime to the repo's install receipt (Task-1 freshness) and REFUSES a stale runtime with a /reload-plugins instruction (workflow commands also fail-closed on an invalid/unreadable receipt, naming /idc:doctor + /idc:update; recovery commands may still expand to migrate), and on a current runtime opens the command's lifecycle record + injects the command-contract context. Stop (second handler): the command-closeout gate — a session that entered a governed /idc:* command but never closed it with a valid terminal status (via idc_command_contract.py finish) is refused the stop, bounded N=3 then loud-fail; it self-selects by the session's own open command records and never clears the record itself. The command entry gate runs on IDC command EXPANSION (matched to idc: commands) — deliberately BEFORE governance, so /idc:init in a not-yet-governed repo is still freshness-checked (a stale runtime is refused; a current runtime is admitted with a bootstrap context that opens no record); every OTHER hook here is repo-gated (a no-op outside an IDC-governed repo). All fail-soft; auto-discovered, so NEVER also listed in plugin.json (the 3.1.1 duplicate-hooks regression).
